title: Nephrodium lanuginosum

taxonomy: Nephrodium lanuginosum belongs to the family Matoniaceae. It is classified as a species of fern, specifically within the phylum Pteridophyta. The genus Nephrodium is part of the order Matoniales, which is a distinct group of ferns. Nephrodium lanuginosum is a type of vascular plant that reproduces via spores. Its classification is a part of the broader botanical nomenclature system.
uses: Nephrodium lanuginosum, also known as maidenhair fern, was traditionally used in medicine for various purposes. In some cultures, its fronds were used to treat fever and rheumatism due to their supposed anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's rhizome was also reportedly used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses. Its medicinal properties are still being studied by modern herbalists and botanists.
